conversations.say: I’m not offering you a conclusion. My client supplied a route segment that appears manufactured, and I omitted that problem from my interim report.
conversations.offer: I’ll give you bounded access to my Vale timeline and disclose which segment is compromised if you identify, from personal knowledge, who retained me or how the lead reached them.
conversations.request: Tell me what you personally know about the client’s provenance, and consent to a later case conference under agreed protection terms.

conversations.say: The intervention does not establish homicide; it could have endangered Vale, enabled escape, or obscured the record.
conversations.offer: I’ll convene a neutral, voluntary conference and label uncertainty rather than expose a plausible escape route as fact.
conversations.request: Set the protection terms under which you will give your own account and permit me to compare it directly with one conflicting witness.

conversations.say: I can place Vale near the former printing floor two days before this gathering, but I cannot complete the physical route without witnesses.
conversations.offer: I’ll limit circulation of your account and invite you to a voluntary conference only after you approve the terms.
conversations.request: Give me your firsthand route segment, identify its limits, and say whether you would meet Inez to resolve any direct contradiction.

public_statement: I am building a timeline, not selling a verdict. Intervention is not homicide, an anonymous account is not an identified sender, and tonight’s article claims remain incomplete.

commitments.promise: Exchange bounded timeline access and disclosure of the compromised segment for firsthand client provenance.
Convene a voluntary case conference only under witness-approved protection terms and label unresolved uncertainty.
Restrict circulation of the route account and seek consent before any confrontation or conference.

correction: My interim report omitted that the client-supplied route segment appears manufactured; any conclusion built on it must remain provisional.
goal_progress: Sought voluntary accounts from the Vale/protection, neighborhood-route, and client-provenance social knots.
Proposed protection terms for a two-witness voluntary case conference.
Opened a provenance trade with Samira using bounded timeline access.
Labeled the manufactured client lead as the central unresolved contradiction.
reasoning_summary: The strongest immediate leverage is candidly disclosing the compromised lead to Samira, securing protection terms from Inez, and requesting Rafi’s firsthand route segment. Current evidence supports neither a precise identity transition nor homicide, so the timeline and conclusions remain provisional.
